name: drive
description: Access, search, and manage files and folders in Google Drive

tools.name: List Files
tools.description: List files and folders in a Google Drive folder or root

tools.name: Get File
tools.description: Get metadata and details about a specific file or folder

tools.name: Search Files
tools.description: Search for files and folders using queries

tools.name: Create Folder
tools.description: Create a new folder in Google Drive

tools.name: Upload File
tools.description: Upload or create a file in Google Drive

prompts.title: Organize Drive files
prompts.prompt: Help me organize files in Google Drive by creating folders and moving files based on file types or names.

prompts.title: Find a document
prompts.prompt: Search Google Drive for a specific file or document by name, type, or content.

prompts.title: Create backup structure
prompts.prompt: Create a backup folder structure in Google Drive and organize important files.

setupGuide.title: Google Drive Integration Setup

steps.title: Create a Google Cloud Project
steps.description: Go to the Google Cloud Console and create a new project

steps.title: Enable the Google Drive API
steps.description: Navigate to APIs & Services > Library and enable the Google Drive API

steps.title: Configure OAuth Consent Screen
steps.description: Go to APIs & Services > OAuth consent screen. Set up your app name, user support email, and developer contact. Add scopes: drive.readonly and drive.file

steps.title: Create OAuth 2.0 Client ID
steps.description: Go to APIs & Services > Credentials. Click 'Create Credentials' > 'OAuth client ID'. Choose 'Web application'. Add authorized redirect URI: http://localhost:3000/api/auth/drive/callback (adjust port/domain for production)

steps.title: Copy Credentials to .env
steps.description: Copy the Client ID and Client Secret to your .env file as GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET

steps.title: Test the Integration
steps.description: Start your application and navigate to /api/auth/drive to initiate the OAuth flow

notes: The same Google OAuth credentials work for all Google services (Gmail, Calendar, Sheets, Drive)
In production, make sure to add your production callback URL to authorized redirect URIs
The drive.file scope only grants access to files created by your app, use drive for full access
You may need to verify your app if you plan to distribute it publicly
